items.briefing:
ESET is warning Australian organisations about two growing threats: criminals using AI to craft more convincing scams, and "quishing" — fake QR codes that redirect victims to malicious websites. Separately, a recent case showed a lone attacker used AI tools to break into a cloud environment within just 72 hours, highlighting how quickly threats can escalate. Small businesses should also be aware of a malware campaign spreading through fake online advertisements that steals passwords and personal data.

On a positive note, Australia's Confirmation of Payee system has now completed over 150 million checks, helping reduce bank transfer fraud. **Practical tip:** Before scanning any QR code — in emails, posters, or messages — verify it comes from a trusted source, and avoid entering login details on any site it leads you to.

featured.title: ESET warns of AI abuse & quishing surge in attacks
featured.quote: AI-powered attacks and QR phishing represent emerging threats requiring immediate Australian awareness.
